headers.label: W
headers.tooltip: W – Win | a pitcher receives a win when he is the pitcher of record when his team takes the lead for good — with a couple rare exceptions. First, a starting pitcher must pitch at least five innings (in a traditional game of nine innings or longer) to qualify for the win. If he does not, the official scorer awards the win to the most effective relief pitcher.

headers.label: L
headers.tooltip: L – Loss | a pitcher receives a loss when a run that is charged to him proves to be the go-ahead run in the game, giving the opposing team a lead it never gives up.

headers.label: ERA
headers.tooltip: ERA – Earned Run Average | represents the number of earned runs a pitcher allows per nine innings (or the length of the game of the category), with earned runs being any runs that scored without the aid of an error or a passed ball.

headers.label: APP
headers.tooltip: APP – Appearance | a pitcher is credited with an appearance if he pitches in a given game.

headers.label: GS
headers.tooltip: GS – Game Started | a pitcher is credited with a game started if he is the first pitcher to throw a pitch for his team in a given game.

headers.label: SV
headers.tooltip: SV - Save | A save is awarded to the relief pitcher who finishes a game for the winning team, under certain circumstances. A pitcher cannot receive a save and a win in the same game. A relief pitcher recording a save must preserve his team's lead while doing one of the following: Enter the game with a lead of no more than three runs and pitch at least one inning; Enter the game with the tying run in the on-deck circle, at the plate or on the bases; Pitch at least three innings.

headers.label: CG
headers.tooltip: GS – Game Started | a pitcher is credited with a game started if he is the first pitcher to throw a pitch for his team in a given game.

headers.label: SHO
headers.tooltip: SHO – Shutout | a starting pitcher is credited with a shutout when he pitches the entire game for a team and does not allow the opposition to score.

headers.label: IP
headers.tooltip: IP – Innings Pitched | measures the number of innings a pitcher remains in a game. Because there are three outs in an inning, each out recorded represents one-third of an inning pitched.

headers.label: H
headers.tooltip: H – Hits | total hits allowed by pitcher.

headers.label: R
headers.tooltip: R – Runs | the number of runs the pitcher has allowed, both earned and unearned.

headers.label: ER
headers.tooltip: ER – Earned Runs | any run that scores against a pitcher without the benefit of an error or a passed ball. Often, it is the judgment of the official scorer as to whether a specific run would’ve scored without the defensive mishap.

headers.label: BB
headers.tooltip: BB – Base on Balls | refers to how many walks the pitcher has allowed.

headers.label: SO
headers.tooltip: SO – Strike Out | occurs when a pitcher throws any combination of three swinging or looking strikes to a hitter. (A foul ball counts as a strike, but it cannot be the third and final strike of the at-bat. A foul tip, which is caught by the catcher, is considered a third strike.)

headers.label: 2B
headers.tooltip: 2B – Doubles Allowed | refers to how many doubles the pitcher has allowed.

headers.label: 3B
headers.tooltip: 3B – Triples Allowed | refers to how many triples the pitcher has allowed.

headers.label: HR
headers.tooltip: HR – Home Runs Allowed | refers to how many home runs the pitcher has allowed.

headers.label: AB
headers.tooltip: AB – At Bats Against | a count of the number of total at bats against a certain pitcher or team.

headers.label: BAVG
headers.tooltip: BAVG – Opponent Batting Average | hits allowed divided by at-bats faced.

headers.label: WP
headers.tooltip: WP – Wild Pitch | a pitcher is charged with a wild pitch when his pitch is so errant that the catcher is unable to control it and, as a result, baserunner(s) advance.

headers.label: HB
headers.tooltip: HB – Hit Batter | times hit a batter with pitch, allowing runner to advance to first base.

headers.label: BK
headers.tooltip: BK – Balk | occurs when a pitcher makes an illegal motion on the mound that the umpire deems to be deceitful to the runner(s). As a result, any men on base are awarded the next base, and the pitch (if it was thrown in the first place) is waved off for a dead ball.

headers.label: SFA
headers.tooltip: SFA – Sacrifice Flies Allowed | refers to how many successful sacrifice flies the pitcher has allowed.

headers.label: SHA
headers.tooltip: SHA – Sacrifice Hits Allowed | ;refers to how many successful bunts the pitcher has allowed.

headers.label: GO
headers.tooltip: GO – Ground Outs | occurs when a batter hits a ball on the ground to a fielder, who records an out by throwing to or stepping on first base. It can also occur when the batter reaches first base — and the defence instead opts to record an out elsewhere via a “fielder’s choice.”

headers.label: FO
headers.tooltip: FO – Fly Outs | occurs when a batter hits the ball in the air and an opposing defender catches it before it hits the ground or fence.

headers.label: WHIP
headers.tooltip: WHIP - Walks and Hits per Inning Pitched | is one of the most commonly used statistics for evaluating a pitcher's performance. The statistic shows how well a pitcher has kept runners off the basepaths, one of his main goals. The formula is simple enough -- it's the sum of a pitcher's walks and hits, divided by his total innings pitched.

headers.label: G
headers.tooltip: G - Games Played | A player is credited with having played a game if he appears in it at any point -- be it as a starter or a replacement.

headers.label: C
headers.tooltip: C – Chances | Represent the number of opportunities he/she has to record an out. The formula for chances is: assists plus putouts plus errors.

headers.label: PO
headers.tooltip: PO – Put Outs | a fielder is credited with a putout when he is the fielder who physically records the act of completing an out – whether it be by stepping on the base for a forceout, tagging a runner, catching a batted ball, or catching a third strike.

headers.label: A
headers.tooltip: A – Assists | Awarded to a fielder who touches the ball before a putout is recorded by another fielder.

headers.label: E
headers.tooltip: E – Errors | A fielder is given an error if, in the judgment of the official scorer, he fails to convert an out on a play that an average fielder should have made.

headers.label: FLDP
headers.tooltip: FLDP – Fielding Percentage | Answers how often does a fielder or team make the play when tasked with fielding a batted ball, throwing a ball, or receiving a thrown ball for an out. The formula is simple: the total number of putouts and assists by a defender, divided by the total number of chances (putouts, assists and errors).

headers.label: DP
headers.tooltip: DP – Double Play | occurs when two offensive players are ruled out within the same play.

headers.label: SBA
headers.tooltip: SBA – Stolen Bases Allowed (Pitcher and Catcher) | number of times a runner successfully stole a base.

headers.label: CSB
headers.tooltip: CSB – Caught Stealing (Pitcher/Catcher) | number of times a runner was caught stealing.

headers.label: SBAP
headers.tooltip: SBAP – Stolen Bases Against Percentage (Pitcher and Catcher) | refers to the frequency with which a catcher/pitcher throw out opposing baserunners who are trying to steal. The formula is simple: CSB divided by total stolen-base attempts (both successful and unsuccessful) against that catcher/pitcher.

headers.label: PB
headers.tooltip: PB – Passed Ball (Catcher) | a catcher is given a passed ball if he cannot hold onto a pitch that, in the official scorer’s judgment, he should have, and as a result at least one runner moves up on the bases.

headers.label: CI
headers.tooltip: CI – Catcher’s Interference | when a catcher (or any other fielder) interferes with a batter at any point during a pitch, the batter is awarded first base if the catcher.
